user_title: COO & Global Head of Change Execution, WPS

talking_points.headline: HSBC Board meets 5 May on Q1 results; market focus on NII and cost discipline
talking_points.why_it_matters: WPS operational efficiency and change delivery sit directly within investor scrutiny of HSBC's cost discipline narrative — any programme delays or cost overruns could attract negative analyst attention when results are published Monday.
bullets: HSBC Holdings confirmed a Board committee meeting on 5 May 2026 to approve Q1 2026 earnings and the first 2026 interim dividend, payable 26 June to shareholders of record on 15 May
Analyst consensus EPS forecast is $2.17 for Q1; investor focus will be on HSBC's at-least-$45bn banking NII, ~1% cost growth constraint, and whether 17%+ RoTE guidance remains credible
WPB wealth AUM growth and private banking revenue delivery will be a key narrative in investor calls, benchmarked directly against DBS and UBS's record Q1 wealth results this week

talking_points.headline: DBS, Deutsche Bank, UBS sweep Q1 with record profits — wealth management leads
talking_points.why_it_matters: All three major HSBC WPB global competitors posted record Q1 profits driven by wealth management strength, setting a high performance bar for HSBC's own Q1 results and reinforcing the strategic importance of WPS change delivery.
bullets: DBS (S$5.95bn record income, S$907m wealth fees), Deutsche Bank (€2.2bn record post-tax profit, €1.8tn AUM), and UBS ($3bn, +80%) all beat analyst expectations in Q1 2026 on wealth and fee income strength
None of the three withdrew their 2026 full-year guidance; all cited Asia Pacific wealth as a priority growth corridor for the remainder of the year despite Iran war and tariff headwinds
Deutsche Bank's earnings highlighted disciplined cost management as central to sustaining 13% RoTE across all divisions — directly relevant to WPS's own cost efficiency and change governance mandate

source_links.title: Deutsche Bank Q1 record results — db.com

talking_points.headline: Standard Chartered's AI Factory governance model: a blueprint for WPS change execution
talking_points.why_it_matters: Standard Chartered's AI Factory — combining platform consolidation, quantifiable KPIs, and risk-first governance across 41 markets — is a directly relevant change execution model for WPS to evaluate as it scales AI globally.
bullets: Standard Chartered has consolidated AI development onto a unified AI Factory platform across 41 markets, applying repeatability principles to standardise governance and reduce deployment friction at scale

The bank measures AI effectiveness with explicit KPIs: financial crime false-positive/negative rates, cyber detection and response times, and A/B-tested client engagement metrics — making ROI trackable at programme level
The AI Factory approach directly addresses consistent governance of AI deployments across diverse regulatory environments — the same challenge WPS faces managing change execution programmes globally

source_links.title: StanChart AI Factory governance — Digital Banker
StanChart risk-first AI scaling — Asian Banker

Competitor Intelligence.title: DBS Q1 2026: Record Total Income as Wealth Management Powers Through Rate Headwinds
Competitor Intelligence.summary: DBS reported record total income of S$5.95bn in Q1 2026, with wealth management fees hitting S$907m and S$10bn net new money, beating analyst net profit consensus of S$2.83bn with S$2.93bn delivered.

Competitor Intelligence.title: UBS Profits Rocket 80% to $3 Billion for First Quarter Beat
Competitor Intelligence.summary: UBS reported net profit of $3bn in Q1 2026, up 80% YoY, with Global Wealth Management contributing $1.97bn profit before tax (+28%) and $37.4bn net new assets as Credit Suisse integration nears completion.

Competitor Intelligence.title: Deutsche Bank Reports Record First-Quarter Post-Tax Profit of €2.2 Billion
Competitor Intelligence.summary: Deutsche Bank posted record Q1 post-tax profit of €2.2bn (+8% YoY) with all divisions hitting 13% RoTE targets. Assets under management rose 9% to €1.8tn on €22bn net inflows.

AI & Technology.title: Standard Chartered and A*STAR to Set Up S$15 Million Lab for Banking AI
AI & Technology.summary: Standard Chartered and A*STAR IHPC announced a S$15m, 3-year AI Banking Innovation Lab to develop portfolio optimisation, fraud detection and NLP tools, including a natural-language query interface for relationship managers.

AI & Technology.title: Standard Chartered's AI Factory Applies the 'Repeatability' Principle
AI & Technology.summary: Standard Chartered's AI Factory uses a repeatability principle to standardise AI development patterns across 41 markets, cutting deployment time and improving governance consistency for high-value use cases.

AI & Technology.title: Standard Chartered Moves into Its Next Phase of Disciplined AI Execution
AI & Technology.summary: Standard Chartered is deepening AI programme governance, consolidating platforms and sharpening measurement practices as it scales high-value use cases across 41 markets using quantifiable KPIs.

HSBC News.title: HSBC: Earnings Release for Q1 2026 and Consideration of First Quarter Dividend
HSBC News.summary: HSBC Holdings confirmed a Board committee meeting on 5 May 2026 to approve Q1 2026 earnings and the first interim dividend, payable 26 June. Analysts expect EPS of $2.17 with focus on $45bn NII and 17% RoTE guidance.

HSBC News.title: Monthly View — May 2026
HSBC News.summary: HSBC Private Bank's Global CIO outlines the May 2026 house view covering asset allocation adjustments amid Iran war risks, quality credit overweights, and Asia equity positioning for private banking clients.

Private Banking & Wealth.title: UBS Q1 2026 Profit Soars 80% on Broad-Based Wealth Growth
Private Banking & Wealth.summary: UBS's Global Wealth Management posted profit before tax of $1.97bn (+28%) with $37.4bn net new assets and 14.4% return on tangible equity, as the Credit Suisse migration of 1.2m clients globally concluded.

Private Banking & Wealth.title: UBS Targets $5 Trillion AUM by 2028 with Strategic Focus on Asia's Growth
Private Banking & Wealth.summary: UBS has updated its invested assets target to over $5.5 trillion by end-2028, with Asia positioned as a key growth engine. Net new asset inflows are planned to accelerate from $100bn to $200bn annually through 2028.

Private Banking & Wealth.title: DBS Q1 Net Profit Beat Underpins 2026 Outlook as Wealth Arm Softens Iran War Risks
Private Banking & Wealth.summary: DBS's Q1 net profit of S$2.93bn beat consensus as the wealth arm recorded S$907m fees. The group head of consumer banking confirmed the bank is 'still hiring on all fronts' across affluent, private client and private banking.

Regulatory & Markets.title: DBS Q1 Net Profit Beat Bolsters 2026 Outlook Amid Iran War Risks
Regulatory & Markets.summary: DBS's stronger-than-expected Q1 results bolster its 2026 outlook, though the CFO noted Iran-related geopolitical risks and tariff uncertainty continue to weigh on Asia Pacific banking conditions and near-term guidance visibility.

Regulatory & Markets.title: Deutsche Bank's Record Profit Masked by Rising Provisions and Capital Concerns
Regulatory & Markets.summary: Despite Deutsche Bank's record Q1 results, rising credit provisions and capital concerns amid geopolitical uncertainty signal broader risk appetite caution across European banking in the current macro environment.

Operations & Change.title: Standard Chartered Launches AI Factory to Unify Innovation and Governance
Operations & Change.summary: Standard Chartered's AI Factory platform integrates data scientists, engineers and business teams in a unified environment to accelerate AI deployment across 41 markets while maintaining regulatory compliance standards.

Operations & Change.title: Standard Chartered's Risk-First Approach to Scaling AI Globally
Operations & Change.summary: Standard Chartered prioritises risk management and governance when scaling AI, deploying explicit KPIs across financial crime detection, cyber response, and client engagement metrics to track AI effectiveness.
